steps.title: Identify two clear points on the line of best fit
steps.content:
From the scatterplot, I can read where the line of best fit intersects the axes and grid:
  • At $x = 0$ hours, the line is at $y \approx 150°\text{F}$
  • At $x = 10$ hours, the line is at $y \approx 600°\text{F}$

steps.title: Calculate the slope using rise over run
steps.content:
The slope of a line is the change in temperature divided by the change in time:
$$\text{slope} = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{600 - 150}{10 - 0} = \frac{450}{10} = 45°\text{F per hour}$$

steps.title: Interpret the slope and match to the choices
steps.content:
The slope is positive (going upward as time increases), so the temperature is increasing.

The rate is 45°F per hour, which is best approximated by 40°F per hour among the choices.

Choices B and D can be eliminated because they say the temperature decreases.

Choice A claims 170°F per hour, which is far too large. That matches choice C — C is correct.
